mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad collapse in consumer demand, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and driving negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above 105.0 signals robust supply-side capacity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion periods yield negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment create stagflationary pressure that forces equity repricing downward as discount rates rise while earnings growth stalls.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, breaking the stagflation transmission mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP levels above 11000 billion chained dollars indicate sustained economic expansion that increases corporate profit expectations, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistical relationship with 5-day forward returns, with p-values exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment falling below 134000 thousand signals labor market contraction that reduces aggregate demand, triggering negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when PAYEMS falls below the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating inflation indicates supply constraints that erode real corporate margins, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns, with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.
